notes:
The biggest update since 0.3.0 — images, encrypted multi-device sync, and an
opt-in AI access layer, plus a wave of editor fixes. (Rolls up everything from
the 0.3.1–0.3.19 dev releases.)

**New**

- **Images** — add pictures to a note by paste, drag-and-drop, the `/image`
  file picker, or by pasting an image URL. Drag the side handles to resize, and
  click to view the full-resolution original in a lightbox. Images are stored
  **encrypted** on disk and their **EXIF/location metadata is stripped** on
  import; a fast WebP preview is generated for inline display. (PRD Addendum 17.)
- **Sync across your devices** — connect an account to keep notes *and images*
  in sync. Includes optional **end-to-end encryption** with a passphrase and a
  printable **recovery key**, key rotation, and **device management** (see and
  remove connected devices). The sync server never sees your note content or
  image bytes. (PRD Addendum 16.)
- **Trash & Archive** — deleted notes go to Trash, restorable for 30 days;
  Archive hides a note from your lists and search without deleting it.
- **Version history** — view and restore earlier versions of a note.
- **Tab continuity** — open tabs are restored where you left off.
- **AI access (MCP)** — opt-in and off by default: let AI tools read (and, if
  you allow, write) your notes through a built-in MCP server, with a read-only
  vs read-write permission setting and an access log that never records note
  content. (PRD Addendum 15.)

**Fixes & polish**

- The caret now lands correctly before a reference at the start of a
  collapsible block; title↔body cursor movement is seamless; and the first body
  line sits consistently for both paragraphs and headings.
- Fixed bullet markers and a stray-bullet glitch in Safari/WebKit (the engine
  the app runs on), and turned off macOS autocorrect/spellcheck squiggles in the
  editor.
- Fixed import/save failures, edit-loss races, and missed search results.
- New app icon and brand mark, a branded loading animation, a working "Show in
  Finder", and dark-mode button hover. Feedback dialog UX fixes.

**Under the hood**

- Monorepo restructure with a Tauri-free storage layer; storage and backup
  hardening. (PRD Addendum 15/16.)
- Encrypted op-log (the foundation for sync + version history) with hybrid
  logical clocks and per-block last-writer-wins, and a content-addressed
  encrypted blob store for attachments.
- Sync service deployed (Hetzner + Supabase EU + Cloudflare R2) with a one-click
  deploy workflow, a multi-user/multi-device sync simulator, and a remote sync
  **kill switch**. Image bytes upload to R2 via short-lived signed URLs — the
  server only relays their existence, never the bytes.
- WebKit end-to-end tests, so editor/rendering regressions are caught in the
  engine the app actually ships on.
